headline: Taylor Sheridan's 'Marshals' Dominates Global Streaming Despite Critical Backlash
lede: Paramount+'s latest Yellowstone universe entry proves audience appetite trumps critical consensus, rocketing to top global viewership rankings.
body:
Taylor Sheridan's controversial 'Marshals' has defied tepid critical reception to become the most-watched streaming series worldwide, signaling the enduring power of the 'Yellowstone' franchise.

The two-part spin-off, which garnered a mere 27% audience score on Rotten Tomatoes, continues to demonstrate remarkable viewer engagement. Industry analysts suggest the show's success stems from Sheridan's established storytelling brand and the loyal fan base cultivated through the original 'Yellowstone' series.

Streaming metrics reveal 'Marshals' has captured unprecedented global attention, outperforming numerous high-budget competitors. The series' ability to maintain viewership despite mixed reviews underscores the complex dynamics of contemporary television consumption.

Paramount+ executives are likely celebrating the unexpected triumph, which reinforces the network's strategic investment in expanding the 'Yellowstone' narrative universe. The show's performance indicates that audience loyalty can overwhelm critical skepticism, particularly in the ever-evolving streaming landscape.
